evals.name: fresh-hostinger-ubuntu
evals.prompt: I just got a fresh Ubuntu 24.04 VPS from Hostinger, 2 vCPU 4GB RAM. Root SSH works with the password they emailed. I'm going to run a long-lived service on it later this week. What's the absolute first thing I should do on the box, and then what comes after that? Give me a runnable sequence.

assertions.text: Runs apt update + apt upgrade as first or near-first step
Creates a non-root service user with useradd -m
Configures UFW with default deny incoming
Disables SSH password auth via PasswordAuthentication no
Warns to verify alternate SSH access before closing port 22

evals.name: harden-running-server
evals.prompt: My DigitalOcean droplet has been running with root SSH and password auth for two months. I haven't been hacked yet but I'm sweating. Walk me through tightening it without locking myself out. I have my SSH public key at ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub on my laptop.

assertions.text: Tells user to copy public key with ssh-copy-id BEFORE disabling password auth
Suggests creating a non-root user before locking down root login
Mentions verifying tailnet/key SSH works before closing public port 22
